moving in the same direction or beside something

vocabulary.meaning: to sit on and control a vehicle or animal

vocabulary.meaning: people living together in an organized community
a level of quality used as a measure

phrases.example: He wants to go further in life.
He loves to ride a bike.
It is situated near the river.

ভালো খাও, অন্যথায় অসুস্থ হয়ে পড়বে।

sentences.en: She rides a horse every day.
The village is situated by a lake.
Set a high standard for yourself.

আমি পরামর্শ দিচ্ছি তুমি আরও পড়াশোনা করো।

quiz.explanation: Advice means a suggestion about what someone should do.
Along means moving in the same direction or beside something.
Attention means the act of focusing the mind on something.
Attract means to cause interest or draw someone toward something.

opts: go up using hands and feet
quiz.explanation: Climb means to go up something using hands and feet.
Drop means to fall or let something fall.
Final means the last in a series; coming at the end.

opts: at a greater distance or degree
quiz.explanation: Further means at a greater distance or to a greater degree.
Imply means to suggest something without saying it directly.
Maintain means to keep something in good condition or continue it.

opts: not one and not the other
quiz.explanation: Neither means not one and not the other of two things.

opts: in a different way or else
quiz.explanation: Otherwise means in a different way; or else.
Physical means relating to the body or natural world.
Prove means to show that something is true.
React means to respond to something that has happened.

opts: sit on and control a vehicle or animal
quiz.explanation: Ride means to sit on and control a vehicle or animal.
Situated means located in a particular place.
Society means people living together in an organized community.

opts: level of quality used as a measure
quiz.explanation: Standard means a level of quality used as a measure.

opts: put forward an idea to consider
quiz.explanation: Suggest means to put forward an idea for someone to consider.

story.title: The Climb That Proved Everything
story.body: Situated on the edge of a wide valley, there was a small school known for its high standard of physical education. The students were taught to maintain focus and pay attention to both their mental and physical health. One day, the teacher suggested a challenge — to climb the hill along the river and return before sunset. Neither the students nor the parents were sure it was a good idea. However, the teacher gave them clear advice: react calmly, help each other, and maintain your standard along the way. A boy named Nabil was the first to attract everyone's attention. He rode his bicycle to the base of the hill, then climbed further than anyone expected. Along the path, he picked up trash to keep the environment clean — a sign that implied he truly cared about society. When others began to drop back from exhaustion, Nabil stopped to give them tips and encourage them to go further. The final moment came when all students reached the top together. The teacher proved that physical effort, the right advice, and a strong society spirit could take anyone to the top. Otherwise, without each other, they would have failed alone.

opts: on the edge of a wide valley
climbing the hill along the river

comprehension.q: What did Nabil do along the path?
What did the final moment prove?
opts: that effort, advice, and society spirit can take anyone to the top

listening.title: Planning a Community Event
listening.script:
A: I suggest we organize a physical activity for the society.
B: That is a great idea. What do you have in mind?
A: We can climb the hill along the river.
B: Neither the elders nor the children have done that before.
A: Pay attention — I have advice for everyone.
B: What does the plan imply exactly?
A: It implies that we maintain our standard and react calmly to challenges.
B: Otherwise some might drop out early.
A: True. We should attract more people by making it fun.
B: Suggest a final date and I will help organize.
A: Let us go further and include food and music too.
B: That will prove to society that we can do great things together.
A: Exactly. A strong community is situated in shared effort.

opts: climbing the hill along the river

comprehension.q: What will prove to society their strength?

speaking.prompt: Talk about one way to improve your society. Use words like suggest, maintain, standard, physical, and attract.
speaking.example: I suggest we maintain clean streets to attract visitors. Physical cleanliness implies social pride. Otherwise, our standard of living will drop. A society that maintains its environment proves that it truly cares.
tips: Mention how it attracts or helps others
End with what it proves about society

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: What do you suggest to improve our community?

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Give a suggestion and explain why.

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: How should people react if they find it difficult?

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: What standard should the community aim for?

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Describe the standard and what it implies for society.
